subject: chemistry
text: Which of the following molecules has a linear geometry?
explanation:
$\text{CO}_2$ has **sp hybridisation** of carbon with no lone pairs → **linear geometry** (bond angle 180°).

$\text{H}_2\text{O}$: bent (2 lone pairs), $\text{NH}_3$: pyramidal (1 lone pair), $\text{SO}_2$: bent (1 lone pair on S).

subject: chemistry
text: The number of $\sigma$ and $\pi$ bonds in $\text{HC}\equiv\text{C}-\text{CH}=\text{CH}_2$ respectively are:
explanation:
Structure: H–C≡C–CH=CH₂

$\sigma$ bonds: H–C (1) + C≡C (1) + C–C (1) + C=C (1) + 2×C–H in =CH₂ (2) + 1 H on terminal = total **7σ**

$\pi$ bonds: triple bond has 2π + double bond has 1π = **3π**
